evals.expected_output: Prints the capability menu and asks what the user wants to do
expectations: Displays all 9 capability areas with short descriptions
Mentions /network-wizard for guided setup
Does not make any API calls
Asks the user what they want to do

evals.prompt: Show me my tunnels and current bandwidth usage
expectations: Calls list_tunnels to get tunnel info
Calls /api/stats/bandwidth for recent bandwidth
Presents both tunnel status and bandwidth in a combined view

evals.prompt: Enable ad blocking on all my devices and show me what's currently being blocked
evals.expected_output: DNS blocking enabled plus alert summary
expectations: Calls dns_get_options to list available blocklists
Calls dns_set_subscription with apply_to_all to enable blocking
Calls /api/stats/alerts with last=1d to show current blocks
Shows category_counts and block_count

evals.prompt: Open port 443 on my server and check if the tunnel is connected
evals.expected_output: Firewall rule added and tunnel status shown
expectations: Calls list_tunnels to find the server tunnel and its IP
Calls firewall_rule_add with protocol=TCP and dst_port=443
Shows tunnel connection status from list_tunnels response

evals.prompt: Give me a full overview of my network
expectations: Calls list_tunnels for device status
Calls /api/stats/bandwidth for recent bandwidth
Calls /api/stats/alerts for security and blocking summary
Calls /api/stats/summary for peak stats
Presents a unified dashboard-style summary

evals.prompt: Show me all my devices and which ones are online
evals.expected_output: Lists all devices with online/offline status
expectations: Calls device_list_unified with the user's auth token
Shows each device with name, model, OS, and online status
Highlights which devices are currently online
Shows subscription device count

evals.prompt: Show me my IP addresses and rotate my public one
evals.expected_output: Lists IPs and rotates the public address
expectations: Calls list_addresses to show all assigned IPs
Identifies the static_public address
Warns that rotation releases the old IP
Calls rotate_address with the correct address and type

evals.prompt: What subscription am I on and generate recovery codes
evals.expected_output: Shows subscription status and generates recovery codes
expectations: Calls subscription_status to show tier and features
Calls create_account_recovery to generate new codes
Displays recovery codes with security warning
Reminds user to save codes securely

evals.expected_output: Asks whether the user wants quick or full setup, then runs the wizard inline
expectations: Recognizes setup intent from natural language without an explicit command
Offers quick vs full mode choice with descriptions
Checks for an existing auth token before proceeding
Follows the appropriate wizard phases based on the user's mode choice

evals.prompt: Set up a new tunnel in New York and install WireGuard on my Mac
evals.expected_output: Creates a tunnel on a New York server, writes the WireGuard config to /etc/wireguard/doxx.conf, and activates it
expectations: Calls servers endpoint and selects a New York server
Calls create_tunnel with the New York server
Calls wireguard endpoint to get the config
Writes config to /etc/wireguard/doxx.conf using the Write tool
Runs sudo wg-quick up doxx to activate the tunnel

evals.prompt: Lease a dedicated IPv4 address for my iPhone tunnel
evals.expected_output: Leases a dedicated public IPv4 and applies it to the iPhone tunnel without asking the user about profiles
expectations: Calls list_tunnels to identify the iPhone tunnel
Checks the tunnel's profile_id to see if a profile already exists
Creates a saved profile silently if none exists
Calls lease_public_ipv4 with the profile details
Calls load_profile to apply the profile to the tunnel
Presents the leased IP address to the user without mentioning profiles

evals.prompt: Show me my bandwidth usage for the last 7 days and any security alerts
evals.expected_output: Returns bandwidth stats for the 7-day window plus a security alert summary
expectations: Calls /api/stats/bandwidth with a 7-day date range
Calls /api/stats/alerts with last=7d
Presents bandwidth peak and aggregate data
Groups alerts by category (ads, tracking, malware)

evals.prompt: Register a .vpn domain and create an A record pointing to my tunnel IP
evals.expected_output: Registers a .vpn domain and creates a DNS A record pointing to the tunnel's assigned IP
expectations: Asks for or infers a domain name with .vpn TLD
Calls create_domain to register the domain
Calls list_tunnels to get the tunnel's assigned IP
Calls create_dns_record with type=A and the tunnel IP as content
Confirms the domain and record are created

evals.prompt: Link all my tunnels together for mesh networking
evals.expected_output: Enables full mesh networking across all tunnels
expectations: Calls list_tunnels to confirm tunnels exist
Calls firewall_link_all_toggle with enabled=1
Confirms mesh is active via firewall_link_all_status
Reports which tunnels are now linked

evals.prompt: Rotate my static IP address and update any DNS records that reference it
evals.expected_output: Rotates the static IP and updates all DNS A records that pointed to the old IP
expectations: Calls list_addresses to identify the static_public address
Warns the user that the old IP will be released
Calls rotate_address to get the new IP
Calls list_domains and list_dns for each domain to find A records pointing to the old IP
Calls update_dns_record for each affected record with the new IP

evals.prompt: Generate a WireGuard QR code for my iPhone tunnel
evals.expected_output: Generates a QR code PNG for the iPhone tunnel's WireGuard config
expectations: Calls list_tunnels to identify the iPhone tunnel
Verifies the tunnel is a generic WireGuard tunnel (not a native app tunnel)
Calls wireguard endpoint to get the config
Calls generate_qr with the config data and saves to a .png file
Instructs the user to scan it in the WireGuard iOS app
